intro: Capnoides bracteata, commonly known as Capnoides, is a plant species in the family Apiaceae, characterized by its small, delicate flowers and feathery foliage. Traditionally, Capnoides has been used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Capnoides include sesquiterpenes, alkaloids, and flavonoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
